changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Wet Blanket"

TextENGNew: For Teppei, coming to Hinamizawa was about lying low until things cooled off.
He would spend at least the summer here, perhaps through the end of the year, and see how things played out.
For a man who could only thrive in gambling dens and entertainment districts, the house in Hinamizawa was practically a hermitage.
changeReason: Old says 'a rather dull hiding place' omitting the context that Teppei could only thrive in gambling dens and entertainment districts; new includes this crucial characterization and translates 隠居 more accurately as 'hermitage.'

TextENGNew: Rina, who had been living with him, had suddenly vanished.
Worse, rumors suggested she had gotten involved in something extremely dangerous.
...Some even said she had already been caught and killed.
Everyone in their circles knew that Rina and Teppei were an item.
So naturally, everyone assumed he was in on whatever she had done.@k Some thought he had swindled a fortune out of the scheme, while others came looking for trouble from dangerous people.
changeReason: Old says 'he got money by tricking her into doing something' (implying he tricked Rina) while new says 'he had swindled a fortune out of the scheme' (people assumed he profited from Rina's scheme); the Japanese describes others assuming he profited, not that he tricked Rina.

TextENGNew: Life in Okinomiya had become very difficult.
More than anything, he was furious to learn that Rina had apparently been scheming with other men behind his back, and that was the real reason he left.
Hinamizawa was the only place left for Teppei to go.
The house where he and his wife once lived had been abandoned ever since they moved into Satoko's house. It was unlivable.
...Besides, he did not even have the key anymore.
But Satoko's house should still be occupied, and it was bigger than the old place--more comfortable.
changeReason: Old adds 'she could clean and cook for him' not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: With such self-serving thoughts rattling around in his head, he made his way back.
The house was locked when he arrived. Assuming Satoko had gone shopping, he wandered through the shopping district until he found her and brought her back.
changeReason: Old says 'dragged her back home' implying physical force, while new says 'brought her back'; the Japanese simply says he found her, without implying he physically dragged her.

TextENGNew: When he made her unlock the door, the inside was thick with dust.
changeReason: Old omits the detail from Japanese 家を開けさせると that he made Satoko unlock the door; new includes this, showing Teppei's controlling behavior of ordering her to open the house.

TextENGNew: She told him she had been staying at a friend's place for the past year, and the house had been left unattended.
Teppei flew into a selfish rage.
changeReason: Old says 'exploded in anger' omitting 自分勝手にも (selfishly); new includes 'selfish rage' which conveys that his anger was unjustified and self-centered, as the Japanese emphasizes.

TextENGNew: He bellowed that it was Satoko's duty to keep the house in order while he was gone.
changeReason: Old says 'told' while new says 'bellowed' for 怒鳴った (to shout/bellow); also new adds 'while he was gone' matching 留守を守る, conveying Teppei's abusive expectations more accurately.

TextENGNew: ...Before long, the expression on Satoko's face had reverted to the one Teppei remembered so well from 1982.
Without a word of protest, Satoko began obeying his every command. First, he ordered her to clean the house.
changeReason: Old omits 文句ひとつ言わず (without a word of protest) and says 'cleaned up the house' as if Satoko acted voluntarily; new captures that she obeyed without protest and that he ordered the cleaning, better reflecting the abusive dynamic.

TextENGNew: A new, spacious, comfortable home.
changeReason: Old adds 'with a new girl' not in the Japanese, incorrectly sexualizing the description.

TextENGNew: For something that was supposed to be just lying low, his new life was off to a fine start.
In high spirits and eager to show off, Teppei called his cronies over for a party at the house.
changeReason: Old adds 'playing mahjong' which is not in the Japanese; the text says 宴会 (a party/banquet). New correctly says 'a party at the house.'

TextENGNew: But his good mood was short-lived.
``I am a teacher at the Hinamizawa Branch School. My name is Chie! Is Satoko Houjou-san here?``
He had thought it was some insurance salesman or solicitor pestering him, but it turned out to be a young woman.
changeReason: Old omits that the visitor was 若い女性 (a young woman); new includes this detail which is in the Japanese and sets up Teppei's reaction.

TextENGNew: And she claimed to be a school teacher.
``Huh? What the hell do you want?@k@vS20/21/442000002. Why should I let you see Satoko?``
changeReason: Old asks 'What do you want with Satoko?' (seeking information) while new says 'Why should I let you see Satoko?' (refusing access); the Japanese 何でおどれに沙都子会わせにゃならんね is a hostile refusal, not an inquiry.

TextENGNew: ``Satoko didn't come to school today. Since we didn't receive any notice, I came to check if something had happened.``
changeReason: Old says 'I wasn't able to get ahold of her' (tried but failed to contact) while new says 'we didn't receive any notice' (no notification from guardian); the Japanese 連絡がなかった means no notice was given to the school, which is more significant as it points to parental neglect.

TextENGNew: ``Huuuh...? School...?``
``Yes. Is Satoko here?@k@vS20/22/441400008. There's something I need to speak with her about.``
``Oh, uh... she's got a cold, yeah, that's right. ...Satoko's sick in bed with a fever...``
``I have some handouts to deliver. Could I perhaps speak with her for just a moment?``

``Are you stupid? Weren't you listening?@k@vS20/21/442000006. Satoko is sick!@k@vS20/21/442000007. She's groaning in bed right now!@k@vS20/21/442000008. How am I supposed to let you see her, you moron?!``
changeReason: Old says 'She's sleeping right now' while new says 'She's groaning in bed'; the Japanese うんうんうなっとる means groaning/moaning in pain, which is Teppei's lie about fever symptoms, not sleeping.

TextENGNew: Inwardly, Teppei realized this was going to be a problem.
It had simply never occurred to him that Satoko needed to attend school.
changeReason: Old adds 'Satoko was just a pet' not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: And to think a teacher would show up at his door just because the girl missed a single day.@k The nerve of it infuriated him.
changeReason: Old says 'This wasn't good' (mild concern) while new says 'The nerve of it infuriated him'; the Japanese 不愉快の極みだ means 'the height of displeasure,' expressing intense anger rather than mild worry.

TextENGNew: Teppei despised schools, teachers, and anyone who acted like they were better than him.
changeReason: Old omits 偉そうなヤツらは全部 (anyone who acted superior/holier-than-thou); new includes 'anyone who acted like they were better than him,' providing fuller characterization of Teppei's resentful worldview.

TextENGNew: ``I see... Do you think she will be able to come to school tomorrow?``
``How should I know?! I won't know until tomorrow comes!@k@vS20/21/442000010. You idiot!!``
``............I... I see. ...All right.@k@vS20/22/441400012. Please give Satoko my regards...@k@vS20/22/441400013. Excuse me...``
changeReason: Old translates 失礼します as 'Thank you' while new correctly translates it as 'Excuse me' (a standard departure phrase); also old says 'tell her that I came by' while new correctly says 'give Satoko my regards' for よろしくお伝えください.

TextENGNew: He managed to chase her off for now, but it was clear that keeping Satoko out of school would invite more trouble.
Teppei intended to stay here for quite a while, so he wanted to avoid unnecessary complications.
changeReason: Old says 'didn't need even more trouble' omitting the reason; new includes that Teppei 'intended to stay here for quite a while' (短からぬ時間を過ごすつもり), explaining his motivation to avoid trouble.

TextENGNew: To make matters worse, someone from the child welfare office showed up that very evening.
changeReason: Old ambiguously says 'he came to the center' but Japanese means a welfare worker visited him.

TextENGNew: ...That female teacher must have reported him.
That bitch. Next time I see her, I'll rearrange her face so badly her own parents won't recognize her...!!
At least Satoko had readily played along, putting on a show of domestic harmony and pretending to be sick when the social worker came...
changeReason: Old adds editorializations ('afraid of him', 'so easy to control her') and omits 円満であること (domestic harmony).

TextENGNew: But with so many eyes now watching him, things were only going to get more difficult from here...
changeReason: Old adds 'He considered Satoko to be a convenient pet' not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``...Aaaagh, goddammit, nothing's going right!!``
